ab: karuwa ku kiwmaumalr kaninu?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: kuwaceng da eman? karuwa ku.
ch: B:有何不好? 我可以啊！

ab: kiumalra ku diya kaninu ay?
ch: A:我訪問你一下好嗎?
en: A: Can I interview you?

ab: yi! adi ’inava a! alra sadu nangku kina’ulidan.
ch: B:喔! 不好吧！因為我有太多不會的。
en: B:Whoa! Not good! Because there's too much I can't do.

ab: miwari u nu ’emanan piya ta kare’alri? maranger ku mukuwa kiwmaumalra kannu, karuwa nay?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: ai, ku lra ngarayaw u i ruma’!
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: miwari u nu ’emanan? maranger ku kiwmalr kannu da vativatiyan na pinuyumayan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於卑南族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I'd like to ask you about the Peinan legend.

ab: yawa ku i henan nu ’emanan. adi ku kaadu i ruma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: kinger ta mu, salaw sadu nanu kinadalram datu kakuwayanan na pinuyumayan, di maranger ku diya kiwmalr kannu.
ch: A:我聽說您對卑南族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Peinan people and I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: adi sadu nangku kinadalram, aymu, saseker ku tumuvang.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: karuwa ku pasirep kananu ikakuda di nanu rinarengay? nu kemani mu, kalre’ayan ku venavalray kikarun.
ch: A:我可以錄下你的動作及說話嗎?這樣比較方便做記錄工作。
en: A: Can I record your movements and words? It's easier to do the recording this way.

ab: ai, nu kemadu.
ch: B:好的，就這麼做。
en: B: Okay, let's do that.

ab: kiwmalra ku diya, pidaya lra nu ’ami harem?
ch: A:請問您今年幾歲了?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: makawalun di pituwa lra ku ’ami.
ch: B:我已經87歲了。
en: B: I'm 87 years old.

ab: yi! mena’u ta marelralrak u diya kananu ’ami.
ch: A:喔！您看起來還比實際年齡年輕啊！
en: A: Wow! You look younger than your actual age!

ab: iya! dawdawan ku lra.
ch: B:哪裡!我已經老了。
en: B: Where! I'm old.

ab: kema i iyan u kana dekalr?
ch: A:您來自哪個部落?
en: A: What tribe are you from?

ab: kemay Kasavakan ku.
ch: B:我來自Kasavakan。
en: B: I'm from Kasavakan.

ab: nanu turuma’an mu, mawu a Pinuyumayan?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是卑南族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Puyuma?

ab: melri, a Amis intaw.
ch: B:不，她是阿美族人。
en: B:No, she's Amish.

ab: sayhu u marengay da pida piningaiyan?
ch: A:您會說哪些語言?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: sayhu ku temaraamis di temarakasavakan.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和建和語。
en: B: I speak Amish and Ken-Hwa.

ab: salaw ku semangal maruwa ku kiwmaumalr kannu harem kana wari, di maruwa u diya venavati kana kakuwayanan nu mucalun ta?
ch: A:今天非常高興能訪問您，然後你還可以講述，有關狩獵的禁忌嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, and can you also tell us about the taboos of hunting?

ab: ai. lralrak ku diya mu, malayas ku muhenan pa’alrupa.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: lrayuwan! nanu ipanadam harem kanini na wari mu, pukasa kana mipauwayan. semangalan!
ch: A:感恩!，您今天所教導的真是太有意義了。謝謝您！
en: A: Thank you! What you have taught me today is so meaningful. Thank you!

ab: nangku rinarengay mu, tatena’ peniya mu! mau tu vinavati pinulrepus na temuwamuwan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的喔！是祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: What I'm saying is absolutely true! It is a discourse handed down by our ancestors.

ab: ini na kiyaumalran mu, ta palruwanay lra kani a! salaw ku semangal kananu vinavati.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這了! 非常謝謝您的講述。
en: A: That's all for today's interview! Thank you very much for your comments.

ab: amana maira, nu aydan venatiya ta lra.
ch: B:不客氣，我們再聊。
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: ai, kayku lra, tingarayaw lra nu adu, lramu kasadu diya nanu vativatiyan.
ch: A:好的，我先離開了，我會期待那時候，也許會有更多的故事。
en: A: Well, I'll leave you to it, I'll look forward to that, maybe more stories then.

ab: ai, tawar lra.
ch: B:好喔!請慢走。
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
